title: Learning from Home: The New Way to Study
passage:
Learning from Home

Today, many students do not go to a school building. Instead, they study at home. This is called online learning or distance education. Sarah is a fifteen-year-old student from Canada. She does not go to a normal school. She studies online at home.

Every morning, Sarah wakes up at 7:30 AM. She eats breakfast and then sits at her desk. She does not need to wear a school uniform. She wears comfortable clothes. At 8:30 AM, she turns on her computer. She connects to the internet and opens her school website. Her teachers upload videos and worksheets every day.

Sarah studies different subjects like English, Math, and Science. She watches video lessons and does her homework on her computer. If she has a question, she sends an email to her teacher. Her teacher, Mr. Davis, always replies quickly and helps her. Sarah likes online learning because she can work at her own speed. If a lesson is difficult, she can watch the video again.

However, online learning is not always easy. Sarah must organize her time well. She cannot play games or watch television during study hours. Also, she sometimes feels lonely. She cannot see her classmates every day. To solve this, Sarah joins an online chat room with her classmates every Friday afternoon. They talk about their hobbies and play online games together.

Let's look at another student, Tom. Tom is eighteen years old and lives in Australia. He lives very far from the nearest city, so online learning is perfect for him. In the past, children in the countryside used the radio to listen to their teachers. Today, Tom uses a high-speed internet connection and a laptop. He has live video classes every Tuesday and Thursday. During these classes, he can see and talk to his teacher and other students on his screen. He says, 'Online learning helps me stay at home with my family and still get a good education.'

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.explanation: The text states: 'Sarah is a fifteen-year-old student from Canada.'

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.text: What time does Sarah turn on her computer?
questions.explanation: The text states: 'At 8:30 AM, she turns on her computer.'

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.text: Why does Sarah like online learning?

options.B: She can work at her own speed.
options.C: She can play games all day.
options.D: She does not have a teacher.

questions.explanation: The text states: 'Sarah likes online learning because she can work at her own speed.'

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: Sarah wears a school uniform when she studies at home.
questions.explanation: The text states: 'She does not need to wear a school uniform. She wears comfortable clothes.'

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: Sarah sends an email to Mr. Davis when she has a question.
questions.explanation: The text says: 'If she has a question, she sends an email to her teacher. Her teacher, Mr. Davis, always replies...'

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE or NOT GIVEN.
questions.explanation: The text lists subjects like English, Math, and Science, but it does not say which one is her favorite.

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: Tom lives in a big city in Australia.
questions.explanation: The text states: 'He lives very far from the nearest city...'

questions.instruction: Complete the sentences below. Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.text: Sarah joins an online ______ with her classmates every Friday afternoon.
questions.explanation: The text states: 'Sarah joins an online chat room with her classmates every Friday afternoon.'

questions.instruction: Complete the sentences below. Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.text: In the past, children in the countryside used the ______ to listen to their teachers.
questions.explanation: The text states: 'In the past, children in the countryside used the radio to listen to their teachers.'

questions.instruction: Complete the sentences below. Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.text: Tom has live video classes on Tuesdays and ______.
questions.explanation: The text states: 'He has live video classes every Tuesday and Thursday.'

vocabulary.meaning: a system of education where students study at home using the internet
making you feel physically relaxed; pleasant to wear
to send programs or documents to a website or computer network
sad because you are alone or do not have friends around you
land outside of cities and towns, often with fields and nature

tactics:
Hướng dẫn làm bài (Test-taking Tips)

1. Tìm từ khóa trực tiếp: Ở trình độ A1-A2, các câu hỏi sử dụng từ ngữ giống hệt trong bài đọc. Hãy dùng kỹ thuật scanning (đọc lướt nhanh) để tìm các từ khóa như '7:30 AM', 'Canada', 'uniform', hoặc 'radio'.

2. Cẩn thận với bẫy True/False: Nếu câu hỏi có chứa thông tin ngược lại hoàn toàn với bài đọc (ví dụ: mặc đồng phục vs. mặc đồ thoải mái), câu trả lời chắc chắn là FALSE. Nếu thông tin không được nhắc tới (ví dụ: môn học yêu thích), câu trả lời là NOT GIVEN.

3. Giới hạn từ: Đối với dạng điền từ, chú ý yêu cầu 'NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS'. Hãy sao chép chính xác từ trong bài, không thay đổi dạng từ (ví dụ: không đổi số nhiều thành số ít).
